blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Taste of East Cobb**
📅 Saturday, May 2 | All day | 955 Johnson Ferry Road, Marietta
🎟️ Varies by vendor | [Learn More](https://tasteofeastcobb.com/)

This is your neighborhood's biggest food party, and it's turning 20 this year. Every restaurant worth knowing in East Cobb sets up shop in one place for one day — from that sushi spot you've been meaning to try to the barbecue joint your neighbor won't stop talking about. It's basically a giant progressive dinner where you can sample everything without committing to a full meal anywhere. The genius part? You discover places you drive past every week but never stop at. Bring the family, bring your appetite, and plan to stay awhile. This isn't just eating — it's East Cobb showing off what makes it home. After two decades, they've figured out the perfect mix of established favorites and newcomers worth knowing. Your Saturday just got a lot more delicious.
scoring_notes: Perfect demographic match - celebrates East Cobb's dining scene for East Cobb residents. Appeals to families and empty nesters alike. 20th anniversary adds special significance. Food costs align with local spending patterns.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Dunwoody Art Festival**
📅 Saturday–Sunday, May 9–10 | Dunwoody
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.discoverdunwoody.com/blog/free-things-to-do-in-dunwoody/)

Mother's Day weekend, Dunwoody-style. The Dunwoody Art Festival takes over the neighborhood every May with local and regional artists, live entertainment, and the kind of outdoor vibe that makes you glad you live where you do. It's free to attend, it's walkable, and it's genuinely a good excuse to get outside with someone you like. If you've been meaning to find something original for the walls — or just want to browse with a lemonade in hand — this is the weekend to do it. Start making plans now.
scoring_notes: Strong demographic fit for the educated, higher-income Perimeter audience — arts festivals over-index with this group. Hyperlocal to the coverage area. Annual event, which keeps the uniqueness score lower, but the Mother's Day weekend timing gives it a natural hook and gifting angle. Free admission removes any barrier. Good forward-looking pick for a newsletter publishing this week — gives readers three weeks to put it on the calendar.
